_note: Married-in spouses (pre-1000 BC batch A) given their DOCUMENTED parents. Only two of the sixteen have parents named in a real source, so only two are filled; the rest are undocumented in scripture and deliberately skipped (no fabrication). (1) MAACAHGESHUR (David's wife) is 'daughter of Talmai king of Geshur' (2 Samuel 3:3); Talmai is himself 'son of Ammihud, king of Geshur' (2 Samuel 13:37), so a father + grandfather chain is added. (2) ELKANAH (father of Samuel) is 'son of Jeroham, son of Elihu' of the Kohathite Levite line living in Ephraim (1 Samuel 1:1, 1 Chronicles 6:27,34 where Elihu appears as Eliab/Eliel), so his father Jeroham + grandfather Elihu are added. Mothers are undocumented (placeholder '—'). No siblings are documented for either. New nodes carry the SPA_ prefix. Sorts after 20a_david_family so MAACAHGESHUR & ELKANAH already exist.

a.label:
👑 Talmai
c.1075 BC · king of Geshur; father of David's wife Maacah & grandfather of Absalom · 2 Samuel 3:3, 13:37

families.note: Maacah is 'the daughter of Talmai king of Geshur' — 2 Samuel 3:3

a.label:
Ammihud
c.1100 BC · father of Talmai king of Geshur · 2 Samuel 13:37

families.note: Talmai is 'the son of Ammihud, king of Geshur' — 2 Samuel 13:37

a.label:
Jeroham
c.1140 BC · father of Elkanah, of the Kohathite Levite line living in Ephraim · 1 Samuel 1:1, 1 Chronicles 6:27

families.note: Elkanah is 'the son of Jeroham' — 1 Samuel 1:1

a.label:
Elihu (Eliab / Eliel)
c.1170 BC · father of Jeroham in Samuel's Kohathite Levite line · 1 Samuel 1:1, 1 Chronicles 6:27, 6:34

families.note: Jeroham is 'the son of Elihu' — 1 Samuel 1:1 (Eliab in 1 Chron 6:27, Eliel in 6:34)
